title: Writing an article for a magazine
explanation:
Writing an article for a magazine

A magazine article should engage the reader with a strong opening, clear sections and a memorable ending.

  • Hook the reader in the first sentence.
  • Use examples and direct address where appropriate.
  • Keep paragraphs short.
  • End with a thought-provoking line.

questions.text: [*Have you ever wondered why some cities feel easier to live in than others?|Ever wondered cities easier live others?|Cities wondered easier live.]
[*In this article, I will explore three features of liveable cities.|Article explore three features liveable cities.|Explore article three features.]
[*First, reliable public transport saves time and reduces stress.|First reliable public transport saves stress.|Public transport first reliable.]
[*Next time you visit a new city, notice how these details shape daily life.|Next time visit city details shape life.|Visit city next time details.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A magazine article often uses a conversational but ____ tone. | *informed | legal | aggressive
Short ____ make articles easier to read online. | *paragraphs | sentences only | footnotes
Examples help the reader relate to the ____.  | *topic | ticket | recipe
A strong title should catch the reader's ____.  | *attention | luggage | salary
[*Another key factor is access to parks and green spaces.|Another key factor parks green spaces.|Factor another parks green.]
[*You might be surprised by how much design affects wellbeing.|Might surprised design affects wellbeing.|Surprised design wellbeing.]
[*However, liveability is not only about infrastructure.|However liveability only infrastructure.|Liveability however infrastructure.]
[*Ultimately, the best cities balance efficiency with humanity.|Ultimately best cities balance efficiency humanity.|Cities ultimately balance.]
